title: Gottlieb Jakob Planck

infobox.value: [Jeremias Friedrich Reuß](/wiki/Jeremias_Friedrich_Reu%C3%9F "Jeremias Friedrich Reuß") [Johann Friedrich Cotta](/wiki/Johann_Friedrich_Cotta_(theologian) "Johann Friedrich Cotta (theologian)")
(15 November 1751 – 31 August 1833) was a German theologian and church historian. He was the great-grandfather of physicist [Max Planck](/wiki/Max_Planck "Max Planck").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

(1785–1831), also professor of [theology](/wiki/Theology "Theology") at Göttingen, published *Bemerkungen über den ersten Paulinischen brief an den Timotheus* (1808) and *Abriss der philosophischen Religionslehre* (1821).[[3]](#cite_note-3)

[The Historical Development of Quantum Theory](https://books.google.com/books?id=W5kyppVPyesC&dq=%22Heinrich+Ludwig+Planck%22&pg=PA31) by [Jagdish Mehra](/wiki/Jagdish_Mehra "Jagdish Mehra"), [Helmut Rechenberg](/wiki/Helmut_Rechenberg "Helmut Rechenberg")

first_paragraph: **Gottlieb Jakob Planck** (15 November 1751 – 31 August 1833) was a German theologian and church historian. He was the great-grandfather of physicist [Max Planck](/wiki/Max_Planck "Max Planck").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

**Gottlieb Jakob Planck** (15 November 1751 – 31 August 1833) was a German theologian and church historian. He was the great-grandfather of physicist [Max Planck](/wiki/Max_Planck "Max Planck").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gottlieb_Jakob_Planck&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Planck was born at [Nürtingen](/wiki/N%C3%BCrtingen "Nürtingen") in [Württemberg](/wiki/Duchy_of_W%C3%BCrttemberg "Duchy of Württemberg"), where his father was a [notary](/wiki/Civil_law_notary "Civil law notary"). Educated for the Protestant ministry at [Blaubeuren](/wiki/Blaubeuren "Blaubeuren"), [Bebenhausen](/wiki/Bebenhausen "Bebenhausen") and [Tübingen](/wiki/University_of_T%C3%BCbingen "University of Tübingen"), he became a lecturer at Tübingen in 1774, a preacher at [Stuttgart](/wiki/Stuttgart "Stuttgart") in 1780, and a professor of [church history](/wiki/Church_history "Church history") at the [University of Göttingen](/wiki/University_of_G%C3%B6ttingen "University of Göttingen") in 1784.[[2]](#cite_note-AD-2)

At Tübingen he wrote *Das Tagebuch eines neuen Ehemannes*. In 1781 he published the first volume of *Geschichte des protestantischen Lehrbegriffs* (History of the Protestant teaching concept); the second volume appeared in 1783, and it was eventually completed in six volumes in 1800. It was followed by an extensive *Geschichte der christlich-kirchlichen Gesellschaftsverfassung* (History of the Christian church's social constitution) in five volumes (1803–1809).[[2]](#cite_note-AD-2)

He died in [Göttingen](/wiki/G%C3%B6ttingen "Göttingen") on 31 August 1833. His son **Heinrich Ludwig Planck** (1785–1831), also professor of [theology](/wiki/Theology "Theology") at Göttingen, published *Bemerkungen über den ersten Paulinischen brief an den Timotheus* (1808) and *Abriss der philosophischen Religionslehre* (1821).[[3]](#cite_note-3)
